message: Can you believe it? That influencer photoshoot is blowing up! We're making waves all over social media.
Our watches and loafers are all over everyone's feeds
You're telling me? My inbox is packed with customer queries

Welcome to Discount Disaster, the responsibility is on you to size up the problem.

? *Use `/help` at any time for a list of useful links and commands*

Once you're ready just click start to begin
Yeah, I'm seeing it, traffic's surging. But nothing we didn't anticipate for
Good. We've optimised the backend for this kind of spike
Let's just make sure that's all it is - legit traffic. Influencer campaigns can attract more than just customers
I'll keep eyes on the dashboard. We're running at nearly 80% capacity right now for all the user actvity our platform can handle
It's all good. People are scrambling to get their hands on our products. That viral post is gold
We're inching closer to max capacity. We're at 95% load now
I just refreshed the website and it's now taking ages to load
Is the website down? Or maybe it's just my machine
getting 503 error on refreshing the site
:star: Reproduces the incident
Its same for me as well
@bob are you getting customer complaints?
:star: Asking questions to validate impact of the problem
Sure let me check the reports
We are getting a lot of calls from customers that they are unable to access the website
Few are reporting long loading times
and few are able to load platforms but unable to order
Here are the latest reports
Looks like a global issue

any recent changes that could be causing this?
:star: Check changes done to the platform
Let me share it with you
https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/external/YmMxM2RhNDA1YWY0NGZmY2E0NzJmN2M5YWJmOGRhOGM
thanks
:bulb: Declare an incident.
*Incident Number INC100013:* Website slowness
@shay is there any chance the load test could have spilled over into prod?
:star: Reviewed changes
This test must be running in UAT
is the test stiill ongoing?
So it shouldn't be impacting prod
Yes its running but in UAT
Is it global issue ?
This will bleed us more due to ongoing sale
@bez We have a major incident, the website is extremely slow to load or in some cases not loading at all. Appears to be load related.
:star: Qualify the problem
:star: Formally assign criticality level to the incident
I see a lot of https426 and 503
given the impact, let's make this a Sev 1
Just to confirm again, would you like me to stop the test ?
@bob any changes in the reports coming in?
@shay yes. stop the test
Reports are turning severe. 180+ complaints so far
Its sale . We can't afford longer downtime
ok stopping the tests now
@bob let customers know we are investigating
Whats going to be the revenue impact ?
Sure i'll keep them posted
180+ customer reports, unsure of revenue impact at this time
Tests have been stopped now
I dont see it making any difference
is there any way to add additional capacity to prod?
Keep me posted and get this resolved asap
ack#
Me and Shay will look into logs now
On capacity, platform team could help more
do we understand the revenue impact?
@hamed @tanya can you explore adding more capacity?
We are losing revenue at the rate of $1.5k every min due to huge sale
Im seeing a lot of errors in the logs
Not sure what the root cause is but we are seeing high load on our Frontend Pods - this may be due to the ongoing discount or if it could be related to any tests we were running.
I see high number of rest-call errors like  http429, http503 and 504s

Do we know how many users we were anticipating in this sale ?
Do we know what the expected amount of traffic should be due to the on-going discount?

logs make it look like single IP address is hitting site a lot
Maya from SecOps would help if you suspect

We are already maxed out on the rack. No additional space is available to add in VMs
Let me run things quickly on my end
Meanwhile lets do traffic analysis
I was looking at Network traffic - But the dashboard took ages to load.
Here's a screenshot of Network traffic across each region.
Have you got any working theories ?
Wow, Spain must really like our products
traffic analysis is being done
Spain's traffic looks very suspicious to me
is it possible to put a geo block in?
Hey @lspesshardt-bloomberg.net, after reviewing the logs, I strongly suspect that this is a security-related issue.
The abnormally high traffic with http 429, 503 and 504 errors may be due to a possible DDoS/DoS attack
I'll need to continue investigating further, but it definitely looks like something is targeting our system
Looks like we have a security threat - We should be extra careful with this incident
Do we know what's the financial impact of the issue? How much are we losing every minute?
suspected ddos being investigated
@lspesshardt-bloomberg.net Should we inform the 'Compliance Team' about this issue?
The Compliance Team makes sure we follow laws and rules, like reporting the incident to the right people and keeping proper records. It helps us avoid legal trouble and protects our reputation.
Keep us informed if it goes beyond hand. Also let us know if you need help from me and Tinus
We dont have geo blocking enabled
is it possible to enable for spain?
@lspesshardt-bloomberg.net do you have suspected IP handy ?
149.34.52.176 from the logs provided earlier
Right now its just a DoS attack. Do you reckon on blocking the single IP first followed by geo blocking enablement ?
@bez unsure about compliance team notification, will need help
Leave Compliance with me - I will talk to them
Shall i start blocking it ?
block single ip
On it
ack#
The IP address has now been blocked
Can you confirm @lspesshardt-bloomberg.net?
Lucky this was DoS (Denial of Service ) from a single IP - What if this attack happens from multiple IP addresses (DDoS)?
I'll keep things ready to enable geo blocking
We should start working on the pending WAF rule updates for the frontend
@lspesshardt-bloomberg.net Should we let the customers know about the attack?
